text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. Instead of adding new features, you focus on making the code cleaner, more organized, and easier to understand. Common techniques include renaming variables, splitting long functions into smaller ones, and removing duplicate code. Refactoring is important because it reduces technical debt—the hidden cost of messy code that slows down future development. When you refactor regularly, you make the codebase more readable for yourself and your teammates, which saves time and prevents bugs in the long run.
